title: excoecaria cochinchinensis var. viridis
intro: Excoecaria cochinchinensis var. viridis, also known as the Poison Tree or the Green Excoecaria, is a plant species found in tropical regions with a history of traditional medicinal use. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria cochinchinensis var. viridis include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in traditional folk remedies and medicine.
